title: Motion In a Straight Line Test - 3 (Topic Test)

questions.questionText: The acceleration experienced by a moving boat after its engine is cut-off, is given by $a = -kv^3$, where k is a constant. If $v_0$ is the magnitude of velocity at cut-off, then the magnitude of the velocity at time t after the cut-off is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is released from rest from a tower of height $3h$. The ratio of times to fall equal heights $h$, i.e., $t_1 : t_2 : t_3$ is
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: The initial velocity of a particle is given by $u$ (at $t = 0$) and the acceleration by $f$, where $f = at$ (here $t$ is time and $a$ is constant). Which of the following relation is valid?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: In the one-dimensional motion of a particle, the relation between position $x$ and time $t$ is given by $x^2 + 2x = t$ (here $x > 0$). Choose the correct statement:
options: The uniform acceleration of the particle is $\dfrac{1}{(x+1)^3}$
The uniform velocity of the particle is $\dfrac{1}{(x+1)^3}$
The particle has a variable acceleration of $4t+6$.
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle having a velocity $v = v_0$ at $t = 0$ is decelerated at a rate of $|a| = \beta \sqrt{v}$, where $\beta$ is a positive constant. After what time will the particle come to rest?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ball is dropped from the roof of a tower of height $h$. The total distance covered by it in the last second of its motion is equal to the distance covered by it in first three seconds. The value of $h$ in metre is $(g = 10\text{ m/s}^2)$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Ball A is released from rest from the roof of a building. At the same time, ball B is projected vertically upward from the ground. At the moment they meet, the two balls are moving in opposite directions, and the speed of A is two times the speed of B. At what fraction of the building’s height does the collision take place?
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A juggler keeps four balls continuously in the air by throwing them one after another. Each ball is projected so that it reaches a maximum height of 20 m above his hand. What should be the time gap between two successive throws so that the balls remain properly spaced? ($g = 10 \text{m/s}^2$)
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: Equation of motion of a body is $\frac{dv}{dt} = -4v + 8$, where $v$ is the velocity in ms$^{-1}$ and $t$ is the time in seconds. Initial velocity of the particle was zero. Then
options: the initial rate of change of acceleration of the particle is 8 ms$^{-2}$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A particle is falling freely under gravity. In first t second it covers distance $x_1$ and in the next t second it covers distance $x_2$ , then t is given by
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A fit young man is standing 6 m away from a building of height 11.5 m. He notices a child slipping down from the top. With what constant acceleration (starting from rest) in $\mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ should he run so that he can catch the child when the child reaches the level of his arm height $(1.5 \mathrm{~m})$ ? Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body freely falling from rest has a velocity $v$ after it falls through distance $2 m$. The distance it has to fall down further in $\mathbf{m}$ for its velocity to become double is :
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A body is projected vertically upwards from the surface of the earth. During its motion, it is subjected to a constant air friction that results in a deceleration of $2 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ always acting against its velocity. If the ratio of the time taken to reach the maximum height to the time taken to return to the starting point is given by $\sqrt{\frac{\alpha}{\beta}}$, where $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are integers, determine the least possible value of $\alpha+\beta$ (Take $g = 10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$).
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A lift starts from the top of a mine shaft and descends with a constant speed of $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} .4 \mathrm{~s}$ later a boy throws a stone vertically upwards from the top of the shaft with a speed of $30 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. If stone hits the lift at a distance $x$ below the shaft write the value of $\frac{x}{3}$ (in m ) [Take: $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ ] (Given value of $20 \sqrt{6}=49$ )
questions.subject: Physics

questions.questionText: A ball is projected vertically upward from the ground with an initial velocity of $u$. At two different times, separated by 6 s, the ball is observed to be at a height of 80 m above the ground. Determine $u$. Take $g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$.
questions.subject: Physics
